error.signature: AI tells a traveler South Africa is visa-free for everyone with no conditions

verdict.summary: Many nationalities get visa-free entry to South Africa (up to 90 days for some, 30 for others); others must obtain a visa in advance, with an e-Visa system for select countries. Passports need adequate validity and at least 2 blank visa pages, and travelers with children face extra documentation rules.

dead_ends.action: Assume visa-free entry applies to every nationality and for a full 90 days
dead_ends.why_fails: Visa-free access and length depend on nationality; some travelers must apply for a visa or e-Visa in advance, and assuming otherwise risks denied boarding.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Visa-free length varies by nationality (30 vs 90 days) and some need a visa.

dead_ends.action: Travel with a passport that has fewer than 2 blank pages
dead_ends.why_fails: South Africa requires sufficient passport validity and at least 2 blank visa pages; insufficient pages can lead to refused entry.

workarounds.action: Check your nationality's rule on the Department of Home Affairs site and apply for the e-Visa if required.
workarounds.how: Confirm visa-free eligibility/length or apply for the e-Visa; verify passport validity.

workarounds.action: Ensure 2+ blank pages and carry required documents, especially when traveling with minors.
workarounds.how: Bring unabridged birth certificates/consent letters for children where required.
